news.title: Robinhood’s venture fund IPO attracted 150,000+ retail investors, CEO says
news.summary: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev says more than 150,000 retail investors joined the fintech’s new venture fund, which offers exposure to private tech companies like OpenAI, Stripe, Databricks, and Oura before they go public.

news.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
news.summary: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

news.title: DOJ says ransomware gang tapped into Russian government databases
news.summary: U.S. prosecutors said a ransomware gang fueled Russian government corruption, and allowed the gang's leaders to avoid paying taxes and dodge the country's military draft.

news.title: AI evaluation startup Braintrust confirms breach, tells every customer to rotate sensitive keys
news.summary: Braintrust, a startup that makes an “operating system for engineers building AI software,” notified customers that hackers broke into one of its Amazon cloud environments, and is asking customers to rotate their API keys.

news.title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas
news.summary: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

news.title: Using AI for Just 10 Minutes Might Make You Lazy and Dumb, Study Shows
news.summary: New research suggests that reliance on AI assistants can have a negative impact on people’s ability to think and problem solve.

news.title: Mexico City Is Sinking. A Powerful NASA Satellite Just Revealed How Fast
news.summary: A new NASA map shows how the sinking of Mexico City is uneven, with areas registering up to 2 centimeters per month.

news.title: Hackers Hate AI Slop Even More Than You Do
news.summary: It's not just you. Scammers, hackers, and other cybercriminals are complaining about “AI shit” flooding platforms where they discuss cyberattacks and other illegal activity.
